# not_constant { #pelage.not_constant }

```python
not_constant(data, columns=None, group_by=None)
```

Check if a DataFrame has constant columns.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code><span class="parameter-name">data</span><span class="parameter-annotation-sep">:</span> <span class="parameter-annotation">[PolarsLazyOrDataFrame](`pelage.types.PolarsLazyOrDataFrame`)</span></code>

:   The input DataFrame to check for null values.

<code><span class="parameter-name">columns</span><span class="parameter-annotation-sep">:</span> <span class="parameter-annotation">[Optional](`Optional`)\[[PolarsColumnType](`pelage.types.PolarsColumnType`)\]</span> <span class="parameter-default-sep">=</span> <span class="parameter-default">None</span></code>

:   Columns to consider for null value check. By default, all columns are checked.

<code><span class="parameter-name">group_by</span><span class="parameter-annotation-sep">:</span> <span class="parameter-annotation">[Optional](`Optional`)\[[PolarsOverClauseInput](`PolarsOverClauseInput`)\]</span> <span class="parameter-default-sep">=</span> <span class="parameter-default">None</span></code>

:   When specified perform the check per group instead of the whole column,
    by default None

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                                                          | Description                                                      |
|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | [PolarsLazyOrDataFrame](`pelage.types.PolarsLazyOrDataFrame`) | The original polars DataFrame or LazyFrame when the check passes |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> import polars as pl
>>> import pelage as plg
>>> df = pl.DataFrame({"a": [1, 2]})
>>> df.pipe(plg.not_constant, "a")
shape: (2, 1)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ a   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ --- в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ i64 в”Ӯ
в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
в”Ӯ 1   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 2   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
>>> df = pl.DataFrame({"b": [1, 1]})
```

```python
>>> df.pipe(plg.not_constant)
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
pelage.types.PolarsAssertError: Details
shape: (1, 2)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ column в”Ҷ n_distinct в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ ---    в”Ҷ ---        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ str    в”Ҷ u32        в”Ӯ
в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
в”Ӯ b      в”Ҷ 1          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Error with the DataFrame passed to the check function:
--> Some columns are constant
```

The folloing example details how to perform this checks for groups:

```python
>>> import polars as pl
>>> import pelage as plg
>>> df = pl.DataFrame(
...     {
...         "a": [1, 2, 1, 1],
...         "b": ["A", "A", "B", "B"],
...     }
... )
>>> df.pipe(plg.not_constant, "a")
shape: (4, 2)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ a   в”Ҷ b   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ --- в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ i64 в”Ҷ str в”Ӯ
в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ A   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 2   в”Ҷ A   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ B   в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ 1   в”Ҷ B   в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

```python
>>> df.pipe(plg.not_constant, "a", group_by="b")
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
pelage.types.PolarsAssertError: Details
shape: (1, 3)
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ b   в”Ҷ column в”Ҷ n_distinct в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ --- в”Ҷ ---    в”Ҷ ---        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ str в”Ҷ str    в”Ҷ u32        в”Ӯ
в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў
в”Ӯ B   в”Ҷ a      в”Ҷ 1          в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
Error with the DataFrame passed to the check function:
--> Some columns are constant within a given group
```
